title: The Canine Mutiny

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer driving
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase perfectly integrated into character, maximum economy

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: *screaming*

jokes.text: Ooh, a free sample of gasoline
jokes.setup: Homer going through mail expecting normal samples
jokes.punchline: Treating gasoline as a normal free sample item
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor that fits Homer's character perfectly - treating dangerous items as mundane

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Ooh, free sample! Don't mind if I do!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... unleaded.

jokes.text: A note from Publisher's Clearinghouse... saying we're out of the running
jokes.setup: Excited tone suggesting good news from Publisher's Clearinghouse
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation - they're explicitly rejected
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke - builds expectation then deflates it efficiently

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh boy, oh boy! A letter from Publisher's Clearinghouse!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I've won! I've finally won!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait... let me read this... 'Dear Homer, you have not won.'

jokes.text: Ich esse. lhr ist. Wir essen. When? When? Ich bin hungrig!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to learn German with educational materials
jokes.punchline: Frustrated outburst mixing English and German about being hungry
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Lisa's academic frustration in a relatable way

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm so hungry, ich bin so hungrig!

jokes.text: Who pumped Ethyl? [Laughing Continues]
jokes.setup: Homer reading his car magazine
jokes.punchline: Crude pun about gasoline that Homer finds hilarious
jokes.explanation: Lowbrow pun that perfectly fits Homer's sense of humor - cheap but character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Heh heh, that's funny. Wait, what? Oh man, I'm gonna tell Barney about this one. Heh heh, crude pun about gasoline. Oh, that's gold, Jerry, gold!

jokes.text: Gas your termites, Freeze your termites, Zap your termites... Save the termites
jokes.setup: Bart reading through junk mail about termite solutions
jokes.punchline: Escalating violence against termites followed by sudden environmental concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - builds violence then subverts with environmental message

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wow, look at all these ways to kill termites! Poison gas, napalm, nuclear weapons...

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, this one says 'Environmentally friendly termite solution.' Hey, we should use this one!

jokes.text: Occupation? Hmm. Butt doctor. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Bart filling out credit card application fraudulently
jokes.punchline: Juvenile occupation choice that amuses him
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bart's juvenile humor while advancing the fraud plot

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: For occupation, I'll put... 'Professional Ass-Kicker'. Heh heh, that's cool.

jokes.text: Income? Whatever I finds, I keeps
jokes.setup: Bart continuing to fill out fraudulent credit application
jokes.punchline: Pirate-like grammar for income description
jokes.explanation: Clever pirate language that's both funny and somewhat accurate for Bart

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Annual income: Arrr, about sixty thousand doubloons.

jokes.text: My name is... Santa's Little Helper
jokes.setup: Bart struggling to come up with a fake name for the application
jokes.punchline: Using the family dog's name as his identity
jokes.explanation: Sets up the central premise cleverly - using the dog's identity drives the whole episode

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I need a fake name... how about... Burp Simpson? Nah, too obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, I got it. I'll use Santa's Little Helper. Perfect!

jokes.text: Who or what is Santos L. Halper?
jokes.setup: Homer seeing mail addressed to the scrambled dog name
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'Santa's Little Helper' that sounds like a real name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'Santos L. Halper' is a perfect anagram that sounds plausibly real

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey, there's mail here addressed to... Snafu's Lil' Kelper.

jokes.text: Thanks, Santos! - [Gags] - A quarter? You're just the dog that keeps on giving, aren't ya?
jokes.setup: Bart kissing the dog in gratitude, then gagging
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about the dog 'giving' him a quarter (probably vomit/drool)
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with sarcastic payoff - Bart's dry reaction to gross dog behavior

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ew! Gross! Thanks a lot, Santa's Little Helper. A whole quarter!

jokes.text: In volume two, Radioactive Man travels through time... to defeat Jesse Owens at the 1936 Olympic games
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy describing a comic book collection enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Heroic time travel used for petty athletic competition against a real historical figure
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - superhero powers used for historically inappropriate purposes, perfect Comic Book Guy delivery

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: The issue where Superman travels back in time to race against Jesse Owens in the 1936 Olympics. Worst. Use. Of. Time travel. Ever.

jokes.text: pardon me, Santos, if that is your real name, Bart Simpson... but your phony credit card is no good here
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy being overly formal with the fake name
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing he knows it's fake and who Bart really is
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Comic Book Guy's superiority complex and attention to detail perfectly displayed

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: Ah, welcome back, Mr. Everyman. Your pull list awaits.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Thanks, uh... Comic Book Guy.

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: That is not your real name, Bart Simpson. Worst. Disguise. Ever.

jokes.text: Now, make like my pants and split!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy kicking Bart out of the store
jokes.punchline: Nerdy twist on 'make like a tree and leave' using pants/split
jokes.explanation: Decent pun variation on classic phrase, fits Comic Book Guy's character

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: Scram, boy! Make like my pants and split!

jokes.text: Our phone doesn't go up to 800! Unless-
jokes.setup: Bart trying to call a 1-800 number
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding how phone numbers work, then having a realization
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's child logic about technology in a believable way

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, hello? I'd like to speak to the manager of 1-800.

dialogue.speaker: Operator
dialogue.line: Sir, 1-800 is just a prefix.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh... so there's no guy named 1-800 running things?

jokes.text: It smells like salmon too. Thanks, boy
jokes.setup: Homer getting golf shirt that supposedly smells like salmon
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious appreciation despite the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic obliviousness to obvious problems

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... smells like salmon. Why would they put salmon smell on a golf shirt?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's disgusting. You can't wear that.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But Marge, it smells great! I'm keeping it.

jokes.text: Let me answer that question with... cookware!
jokes.setup: Marge asking where Bart got all his expensive gifts
jokes.punchline: Bart deflecting the serious question by presenting more gifts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart deflection technique - avoiding trouble with more impressive distractions

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Bart, where did you get all these expensive gifts?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Uh, well Mom, I can explain... but first, I got you something!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ta-da! A new purse! And Homer, this deluxe thermos is for you. Lisa, I know how much you love the environment, so I got you these solar-powered earmuffs.

jokes.text: You a winner! Ha-ha-ha! You a winner! Ha-ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Bart's frying pan radio playing
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical Japanese game show enthusiasm from kitchen appliance
jokes.explanation: Relies on cultural stereotypes, somewhat dated humor but fits the absurd catalog items

dialogue.speaker: Japanese Radio Voice
dialogue.line: Hai! Welcome to Kitchen Appliance Championship! Your frying pan is doing VERY well! Sugoi! *ding ding ding* Ten thousand points!

jokes.text: You've been all edgy and suspicious ever since I gave you those pep pills
jokes.setup: Marge being paranoid about Bart's spending
jokes.punchline: Revealing the source is the 'Trucker's Choice' stay-alert capsules from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that explains Marge's behavior while advancing the plot

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Bart, I'm worried about how much money you've been spending. Where is it all going?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba, Mom, I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Don't lie to me. I found these empty bottles hidden in your room.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh, those? Those are just Trucker's Choice stay-alert capsules. I needed them to stay awake during school.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Stay-alert capsules? Bart, those are basically speed!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well, that explains why I've been so jittery and why I reorganized the garage at 3 AM.

jokes.text: If I want to comment on it, I'll comment on it. Who's gonna stop me? You, Pep Pill Boy? Pep Boys, pills, Beverly Sills
jokes.setup: Marge defending her suspicious behavior
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire word association showing the effects of stimulants
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Marge on stimulants - rapid word association is perfectly executed

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I don't know what you're talking about! Coffee? I barely drink it!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Word association! Ready? Relax.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Tired.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Bed.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Lonely.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sexy.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Flat tire.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Quattro!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Forbidden.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Fruit!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Ugh, never mind.

jokes.text: Only 800 will be bred. Quite possibly the world's best dog
jokes.setup: Reading catalog description of expensive designer dog
jokes.punchline: Absurdly limited production and grandiose claims for a dog
jokes.explanation: Satirizes marketing language for luxury items - treating dogs like limited collectibles

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wow, check this out. 'Only 200 of these dogs are made each year, ensuring exclusivity and prestige.'

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: 'Each puppy is hand-selected by our team of expert geneticists and raised in a climate-controlled environment with individual attention from a personal trainer.'

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: 'This breed has been scientifically proven to increase your social status by 47 percent.'

jokes.text: Twelve hundred bucks! I better just get one
jokes.setup: Bart seeing the expensive price of the designer dog
jokes.punchline: Acting like he's being financially responsible by limiting himself to one
jokes.explanation: Perfect child logic - treating $1200 as reasonable if you only buy one. Great character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I'll just get the one designer dog. That's being financially responsible.

jokes.text: Hello. My name is Laddie. I've been trained to gather fruit for you as a welcome gift
jokes.setup: New super-trained dog arriving at the Simpson house
jokes.punchline: Dog speaks with perfect articulation and has oddly specific training
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor - talking dog with impeccable manners and bizarre specific skills

dialogue.speaker: Laddie
dialogue.line: Good evening. I am Laddie, a purebred collie of impeccable pedigree and breeding.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, a talking dog!

dialogue.speaker: Laddie
dialogue.line: I have been trained in advanced obedience, French cuisine, and interpretive dance.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Can you do anything cool?

dialogue.speaker: Laddie
dialogue.line: I can recite the periodic table of elements and perform advanced calculus.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: That's... oddly specific training for a dog.

dialogue.speaker: Laddie
dialogue.line: My previous owner was a theoretical physicist. He had very particular requirements.

jokes.text: That's Laddie. Bart says he won him at a church carnival two towns over. In a truth-telling contest, right, Bart?
jokes.setup: Marge explaining where the expensive dog came from
jokes.punchline: Ironic 'truth-telling contest' when Bart is obviously lying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - describing Bart winning a 'truth-telling contest' while he's committing fraud

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: We got him from a fancy breeder. He cost $3,000.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Whoa! That's like, a lot of money, man.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Yes, well, your father insisted. Now, Bart, I want you to tell me the truth about something.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Uh, sure, Mom.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Did you break the Ming vase?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: No way! I was at Nelson's house the whole time. We were, uh, feeding orphans and helping old ladies cross the street.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's wonderful, Bart. You're being so honest.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Thanks, Mom. I always tell the truth.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. A dog like this you have to feed every day
jokes.setup: Reading instructions about caring for the expensive dog
jokes.punchline: Homer treating daily feeding as an unusual requirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating basic pet care as a revelation about fancy dogs

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Let's see... feed the dog every day? Every day?!

jokes.text: Some call it the dog that never sleeps... though it actually does while jogging
jokes.setup: Reading more dramatic description of the super dog
jokes.punchline: Grandiose claim immediately undermined by practical clarification
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - builds up mythical dog status then deflates it with absurd detail

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A super dog, you say? Amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: Yes, with incredible powers beyond imagination.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow! What can he do?

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: He can leap tall buildings in a single bound, fly faster than a speeding bullet, and possess the strength of a hundred men!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Unbelievable!

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: Although, he does need to stop for bathroom breaks every twenty minutes.

jokes.text: [Toilet Flushing] - Whoa! - Wow
jokes.setup: Family wondering where the amazing dog went
jokes.punchline: Dog is using the toilet like a human
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with perfect economy - dog's sophistication shown through toilet use

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Where's Santa's Little Helper?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I don't know. I haven't seen him all morning.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: That's strange. He never leaves without telling us.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba! Look in the bathroom!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What the—? Santa's Little Helper, what are you doing?!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh my stars! He's using the toilet like a person!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Remarkable. He's actually figured out how to use the facilities.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I'll be. Our dog's got better bathroom etiquette than I do.

jokes.text: Good dog
jokes.setup: Credit card company calling to threaten Bart about unpaid bills
jokes.punchline: Bart hangs up and praises the dog as if it solved the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan - Bart treating avoidance as problem-solving, praising dog for coincidental timing

dialogue.speaker: Credit Card Company
dialogue.line: We're calling about your unpaid bills.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh man, I can't deal with this right now.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: *hangs up phone*

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Good boy, Santa's Little Helper! You really came through for me!

jokes.text: We didn't see nothin' if you didn't see nothin'
jokes.setup: Repo men arriving to take back Bart's fraudulent purchases
jokes.punchline: Repo men acting like criminals despite doing legal work
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - repo men talking like mobsters for routine legal work

dialogue.speaker: Repo Man 1
dialogue.line: We're here for the merchandise.

dialogue.speaker: Repo Man 2
dialogue.line: Hand it over, kid.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: You guys are like criminals!

dialogue.speaker: Repo Man 1
dialogue.line: We prefer 'aggressive entrepreneurs.'

dialogue.speaker: Repo Man 2
dialogue.line: Now where's the stuff?

jokes.text: Hey, stop! You can take back the bassinet, but the baby's ours!
jokes.setup: Repo men taking back all of Bart's fraudulent purchases
jokes.punchline: Bart defending against them taking something they never sold
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Bart's desperation leads to defending things that aren't even at risk

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey, you can't take that! You guys never sold me that!

dialogue.speaker: Repo Man
dialogue.line: We don't care, kid. If it's in the house, it goes.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But my mom bought this one!

jokes.text: Sorry, boy. I didn't plan this. It just sort of happened
jokes.setup: Bart being forced to give up the expensive dog to repo men
jokes.punchline: Bart's weak excuse that makes him sound like a criminal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's inability to take responsibility even when caught

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, wait! You can't take Santa's Little Helper! He's part of the family!

dialogue.speaker: Repo Man
dialogue.line: Son, your father hasn't made a payment in six months.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But... but I can explain! He just wandered into our yard one day, and I swear I didn't know he was stolen!

jokes.text: Do you think he noticed?
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer is wearing a tie to impress the dog
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely caring about the dog's opinion of his appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer insecurity - desperately wanting approval from a dog

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, are you wearing a tie to impress the dog?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, yeah. What if he thinks I look ridiculous?

jokes.text: We wrecked the first dog. We've gotta treat this one right
jokes.setup: Explaining why they put Santa's Little Helper in a kennel
jokes.punchline: Treating pet ownership like they broke the first one
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating pets like appliances that can be 'wrecked' - perfect Simpson family logic

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: We're just gonna get another dog. It's like when my parents treat pet ownership like they broke the first one.

jokes.text: How come we never take Santa's Little Helper here? - Crowds give him gas
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why they never brought their old dog to the nice park
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact explanation about dog's digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of crude but believable dog behavior explanation

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Why didn't we ever bring Santa's Little Helper to the nice park?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, Lisa, every time we took him there, he'd get the runs right in the middle of the playground.

jokes.text: No, no. Jessica's been fixed. She just needs a little- [Clicks Tongue] attention
jokes.setup: Kent Brockman asking if Laddie is available for breeding
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's not about breeding but about his female dog's sexual needs
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor about pet sexuality that fits Kent's character as somewhat inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: Is Laddie available for breeding?

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, he's a boy dog, so...

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: Oh, I know that. It's my female dog who needs it.

jokes.text: Why, that canine has the proud bearing and glossy fur coat of a Yale man
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns observing the fancy dog
jokes.punchline: Comparing dog qualities to Ivy League stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - his obsession with elite class markers applied to dogs

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Ah, what a magnificent beast. Look at that pedigree, that breeding. Why, this dog is practically Ivy League material.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Indeed, sir. A fine specimen.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, yes. And like all Ivy Leaguers, it's completely useless and costs a fortune to maintain.

jokes.text: Smithers, I believe this dog was in Skull and Bones
jokes.setup: Burns continuing to assess the dog's elite credentials
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the dog belonged to Yale's secret society
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Burns' elite obsession - from Yale student to secret society member

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Excellent pedigree. Yale man, are you? Skull and Bones, perhaps? I thought so. The way you carry yourself, the subtle air of entitlement... You have the bearing of someone who's been to the right schools and knows all the right people. Or should I say, the right dogs.

jokes.text: Remember the time he ate my goldfish, and you lied to me and said I never had any goldfish? But why did I have the bowl, Bart? Why did I have the bowl?
jokes.setup: Lisa defending Santa's Little Helper against criticism
jokes.punchline: Revealing a specific traumatic incident where Bart gaslighted her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts as defense, becomes accusation, ends with perfect logical question

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Santa's Little Helper is a good dog. He's never hurt anyone.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What about the time he ate your saxophone?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: That was YOU, Bart! You broke it and blamed the dog!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: No way, man. Everyone saw the dog do it.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I saw YOU do it! I was right there! Why does no one ever believe me?

jokes.text: Lumley, shovel on more dogs, won't you? Aye, aye. More dogs!
jokes.setup: Bart imagining worst case scenario for Santa's Little Helper
jokes.punchline: Dogs being used as fuel in steamship
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor typical of Simpsons fantasy sequences

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh no! What if Santa's Little Helper ends up being used as fuel in a steamship?!

dialogue.speaker: Bart's imagination
dialogue.line: Dogs being shoveled into the furnace of a steamship to keep it running...

jokes.text: I'd take him, but those cards are nontransferable
jokes.setup: Homer being asked to walk Laddie
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking dog walking requires transferable cards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - misunderstanding dog ownership as requiring official documentation

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Walk Laddie? But I don't have a dog-walking license.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Don't you need some kind of transferable card for that?

jokes.text: And, Baby Gerald... we can't help but wonder what mischief you'll get into next
jokes.setup: News report about Laddie's heroic rescue
jokes.punchline: Ominous ending treating baby as potential troublemaker
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of news reporting style that blames victims or creates false drama

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We interrupt this program to bring you a special news report. A heroic dog named Laddie has rescued a baby from a well.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: The baby is safe, thanks to this brave canine. However, authorities are concerned about the child's future criminal potential.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We'll continue to monitor this situation closely. Back to you.

jokes.text: Boy, we sure could use a dog like that on the force!
jokes.setup: Police witnessing Laddie's competence
jokes.punchline: Police admitting they need help from a dog
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing police incompetence through their admiration of dog

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: We need all the help we can get.

dialogue.speaker: Officer Lou
dialogue.line: Yeah, even if it's from a dog.

jokes.text: You know how I feel about giving!
jokes.setup: Homer learning Bart gave both dogs away
jokes.punchline: Homer having a philosophical opposition to giving in general
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - turning specific situation into broader selfish philosophy

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You gave away both dogs? Why would you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: They needed a good home, Dad.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And what's wrong with this home? We're not good enough? I'm philosophically opposed to giving. It's like losing, but it confuses people.

jokes.text: So you can either sit there crying and eating can after can of dog food... until your tears smell enough like dog food to make your dog come back
jokes.setup: Homer giving Bart advice about getting his dog back
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and illogical plan involving dog food consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Homer's completely insane but internally consistent advice

jokes.text: Rats! I almost had him eatin' dog food
jokes.setup: Bart deciding to go find his dog instead of eating dog food
jokes.punchline: Homer disappointed he couldn't manipulate Bart into eating dog food
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy revealing Homer's true motivations - he actually wanted to see Bart eat dog food

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! I was really looking forward to tricking Bart into eating dog food.

jokes.text: But I don't want to!
jokes.setup: Repo man telling customer to pay bills next time
jokes.punchline: Customer's childish refusal to pay bills
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - adult using child logic to avoid responsibility

dialogue.speaker: Repo Man
dialogue.line: You should pay your bills next time.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Why should I?

jokes.text: Yeah, I bought your mutt, and I ate him! I 'ate his little face, I 'ate his guts... and I 'ate the way he's always barking!
jokes.setup: Bart asking Willie if he bought the dog
jokes.punchline: Willie using 'ate/'hate wordplay to confuse about the dog's fate
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that creates temporary horror before revealing it's just Willie's accent

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Did you buy the dog, Willie?

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: Aye, I did buy the dog. But then I ate it.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: You ate it?!

dialogue.speaker: Groundskeeper Willie
dialogue.line: Nay, nay, I hate it. Hate it so much I could eat it.

jokes.text: Oh, I see. You hate him, so you gave him to the church
jokes.setup: Willie explaining he gave the dog to the church
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact summary of Willie's logic
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction - Bart treating Willie's hatred as normal motivation for charity

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight. You gave our dog to the church because you thought it would be safer there than at home with us.

jokes.text: Yes, I remember Satan's Little Helper... littering the rectory with his dirt, biting me in the apse
jokes.setup: Reverend describing the dog's bad behavior at church
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing dog's name and using 'apse' (church part) for 'ass'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay using church architecture terms for body parts - perfect for religious character

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Yes, well, the problem is that your dog, uh... Santas Little Helper, has been, uh, defecating in the apse.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Defecating in the ass?

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: No, no, the apse. It's a part of the church.

jokes.text: You didn't crucify him?
jokes.setup: Reverend saying the dog is 'no longer among us'
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate assumption about church methods
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Bart immediately jumping to worst-case scenario about church methods

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: The dog is no longer among us.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Did you guys excommunicate him?

jokes.text: Especially since my parrot decided to stop talking. Polly? Polly, want to say hi to our guest? Huh? Fine. Be that way
jokes.setup: Blind man explaining his relationship with his dog
jokes.punchline: One-sided conversation with unresponsive parrot
jokes.explanation: Sympathetic character comedy showing blind man's loneliness and one-sided pet relationships

dialogue.speaker: Blind Man
dialogue.line: Well, my dog and I have a very special relationship. He understands every word I say.

dialogue.speaker: Blind Man
dialogue.line: We talk about everything - the weather, politics, his day...

dialogue.speaker: Blind Man
dialogue.line: Of course, he never talks back. But that's what makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Blind Man
dialogue.line: You know, I tried the same thing with a parrot once.

dialogue.speaker: Blind Man
dialogue.line: Same one-sided conversation, but somehow it felt lonelier.

jokes.text: I can't promise I'll try, but I'll try to try
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Bart not to freak out the blind man
jokes.punchline: Bart's overly complicated non-commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - finding ways to avoid making real commitments through wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, promise me you won't freak out the blind man.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I promise that I will endeavor to refrain from any untoward behavior that might cause undue psychological distress to the aforementioned visually impaired gentleman, provided said gentleman does not himself engage in behavior which I deem to be of a disagreeable or otherwise disturbing nature.

jokes.text: Ha! I may be blind, but you just ran into a closet
jokes.setup: Bart trying to escape from the blind man
jokes.punchline: Blind man successfully trapping sighted person
jokes.explanation: Good irony - blind man outsmarting sighted person through superior knowledge of his environment

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Help! Help! This blind guy's got me!

dialogue.speaker: Blind Man
dialogue.line: Ha ha! You can't escape me, boy!

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: But I can see! I have all the advantages!

dialogue.speaker: Blind Man
dialogue.line: Not anymore, you don't!

jokes.text: Tell you what- Why don't we let the dog decide?
jokes.setup: Mitchell and Bart both wanting the dog
jokes.punchline: Reasonable solution to let dog choose its owner
jokes.explanation: Simple but fair solution showing Mitchell's reasonable nature

dialogue.speaker: Mitchell
dialogue.line: Well, I suppose the reasonable solution is to let the dog choose its owner.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's fair.

jokes.text: Why, without it I could, uh, go even blinder, right?
jokes.setup: Mitchell trying to justify his marijuana possession as medical
jokes.punchline: Claiming he could become more blind than he already is
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - treating blindness as a condition that could worsen further

dialogue.speaker: Mitchell
dialogue.line: I need this for medical purposes. Without it, I could become even more blind than I already am.

jokes.text: That cat's been struttin' around like she owns the place
jokes.setup: Bart and Santa's Little Helper returning home
jokes.punchline: Setting up the classic dog vs. cat conflict
jokes.explanation: Standard pet rivalry setup - functional but not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey, Santa's Little Helper, meet Snowball II.

dialogue.speaker: Santa's Little Helper
dialogue.line: *barks and growls*

dialogue.speaker: Snowball II
dialogue.line: *hisses*

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Aw, come on you guys, can't we all just get along?

dialogue.speaker: Santa's Little Helper
dialogue.line: *chases Snowball II*

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Doh!

tmdb_overview: When Bart can't pay the credit card bill for his expensive new purebred dog, the repo men unwittingly take Santa's Little Helper.
insight: Dog-centered chaos generates 2.76 jokes per minute, but 71.6 score reveals uneven landing.
